doc.title: Lessee Finance Lease — Commencement
doc.event: Ramos Co. signs a 5-year lease for equipment with a 5-year useful life; ownership transfers to Ramos at the end of the term (finance lease). Annual payments are $50,000 and the 8% implicit rate is known to Ramos (PV factor, ordinary annuity, 8%, 5 periods = 3.99271). Prepare Ramos's entries at lease commencement, including any payment made that day.

lines.why: Capitalize the PV of the payments — annuity-due factor because payments start immediately.
lines.trap: Annuity due = ordinary factor × (1 + i). Missing the ×1.08 understates the asset by ~$16,000.

lines.why: The obligation equals the same PV at commencement.

entries.label: First payment — made the same day

lines.why: A day-one payment is ALL principal — zero time has passed, so zero interest.
lines.trap: No Interest Expense line here. Interest needs time on the clock.

lines.why: Ordinary-annuity PV — first payment is a full year away.
Same PV. Interest will accrue on the full balance for year one.

memorize.body: Beginning-of-period payments = annuity due: ordinary factor × (1 + i).
ROU amortizes over the ASSET's life when ownership transfers or a purchase option is reasonably certain; otherwise the shorter of lease term or asset life.

questions.prompt: Payments of $50,000 due at the BEGINNING of each of 5 years, 8% rate (ordinary factor 3.99271). Initial lease liability = ?

distractors.feedback: You used the ordinary-annuity factor. Beginning-of-year payments are an annuity due — multiply by 1.08.
That's the gross payments (5 × 50,000). Capitalize the PRESENT VALUE, not the undiscounted total.

build.scaffold: Entry 1 sets up ROU = Liability = PV. Entry 2 (if payment is due today): all principal, no interest.
